RCN4 VS Other RCNs

This plot is used to check the immune celltype proportion differences between 2 RCNs

The Fisher test is used to calculate the P values

The darker the color, the greater the difference

The larger the bubble, the higher the significance

Bubble plot

RCN4 VS Other RCNs

With the comparison of RCN4 (VIM+.Stroma Rich) and other RCNs, almost all results show RCN4 has higher proportion of CD4.T.cells and CD206.Macrophages, except RCN4 VS RCN6, because RCN6 is CD4.T.cell rech neighborhood.

Bubble plot

Bubble plot

RCN8 VS Other RCNs

We get different results from these comparison, maybe because RCN8 is CD206.Macrophages rich neighborhood, it has low proportion of other immune celltypes.

Scimap spatial interaction

This is done with radius = 45px per image

UMAP

Here I only use celltype markers, but scaled data. The batch effect from T2, 1B and INF2.


celltype markers ('E.Cadherin','Vimentin','SMA', 'panCK','CD8a', 'CD4', 'CD68', 'CD206', 'FOXP3', 'CD45', 'CellID', 'imageid')

Summary of this period

After quality control, we have 3.6M cells in 345 TMA cores of 9 images from 186 patients.

TMA cores were annotated by a pathologist to identify key morphological regions, including the tumor center, invasive border, inflammation, and lymph node metastases.

The CyCIF data was divided into 10 RCNs based on 10 celltypes by using Scimap spatial count (radius = 45 px (30 microns) and k-means = 10)

Antioxidant markers (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1) expression within different RCNs
GCLC has higher expression in RCN4 (VIM+.stroma), RCN6 (CD4.T.cells), RCN8 (CD206.Macrophages), and RCN9 (CD26.Macrophages) rich neighborhoods.

Antioxidant markers (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1) expression within different celltype_annotation
Antioxidant markers are concentrated in the inflammation locations.
In other areas, GCLC has high expression in immune cells.

In tumor cells, GCLC has higher correlation with Vimentin.

RCN4 and RCN8 have relative higher proportion of Tumor_GCLC+VIM+ cells
Compared with other RCNs one by one, RCN4 has more CD4.T.cells and CD206.Macrophages. But RCN8 only has more CD206.Macrophages, because it’s CD206.Macrophages rich neighborhoods.
Compared with other RCNs at the same time, the results will be influenced by the cell numbers.

Scimap spatial interaction, the Tumor.GCLC+VIM+ did show high interact with immune celltypes.

th other RCNs at the same time, the results will be influenced by the cell numbers.

Scimap spatial interaction, the Tumor.GCLC+VIM+ did show high interact with immune celltypes.

UMAP, I tried different ways to do UMAP, but big batch effect. Raw data?

Next step

RCN4 and RCN8 have relative higher proportion of Tumor_GCLC+VIM+ cells
Compared with other RCNs one by one, RCN4 has more CD4.T.cells and CD206.Macrophages. But RCN8 only has more CD206.Macrophages, because it’s CD206.Macrophages rich neighborhoods.
Compared with other RCNs at the same time, the results will be influenced by the cell numbers.
Randomly select from other RCNs group, get the same cells between these 2 groups.
Calculate the radio: Tumor_GCLC+VIM+ / Tumor proportion in RCN

Scimap spatial interaction, the Tumor.GCLC+VIM+ did show high interact with immune celltypes.
Try Giotto: 
findInteractionChangedFeats: Identifies cell-to-cell Interaction Changed Features (ICF), i.e. features that are differentially expressed due to proximity to other cell types

UMAP, I tried different ways to do UMAP, but big batch effect.
Raw data and R

DEG analysis of the scRNA-seq data 🆘
